text.text:
If you take your pastries seriously, Artisanal Goods by CAR needs to be on your radar immediately. This little gem on Colorado Blvd in Pasadena is turning out some of the finest baked goods I've had anywhere in LA.
The Chocolate Croissant is a masterpiece. The chocolate filling is perfectly sweet — rich but not cloying — and the crust is the flakiest, most buttery, most satisfying crunch I have ever experienced in a croissant. Layers upon layers of golden perfection. This alone is reason enough to make the trip. The Almond Croissant was equally stunning — delicate, subtly sweet, topped with sliced almonds and a dusting of powdered sugar that made it look almost too pretty to eat. Almost. The Kouign Amann was buttery and delicate with that signature caramelized crunch that makes this pastry so addictive. Every single item was executed at a level that rivals anything you'd find at a high-end Parisian bakery.
Wash it all down with their cappuccino — bold, strong, and the perfect pairing for the richness of the pastries. Not for the faint of heart but exactly what a proper cappuccino should be.
The atmosphere matches the quality of the food — chill and relaxed with seating both inside and out, the kind of place you want to linger and take your time. The staff are warm and genuinely happy to be there, which makes the whole experience even better.
Yes, pastries run $6–$8 each, but when the quality is this exceptional you won't think twice. This is one of the best bakeries in the San Gabriel Valley, full stop. Go early before they sell out.

text.text:
Such a cute spot and some of the best croissants I’ve had! They were out of the almond croissants but I ordered butter, chocolate, and turkey bacon
I had an iced coffee but can’t wait to try their hot lattes, will be coming back to try their other menu items !

originalText.text:

I had an iced coffee but can’t wait to try their hot lattes, will be coming back to try their other menu items !

text.text:
I stopped by this café and it was absolutely worth it. I ordered the Madagascar vanilla latte, which was really nice — smooth, comforting, and perfectly balanced without being overly sweet. I also tried the plain croissant, and it honestly ended up being one of the best croissants I’ve ever had. It was incredibly buttery and flaky, with light, airy layers and just the right crisp on the outside.

There was a bit of a line, so you might have to wait, but once you get your order, it totally makes sense why it’s popular. If you’re into great coffee and truly amazing pastries, this place is a must-stop.

text.text: Just discovered the BEST croissants at this small bakery! 🥐🤩 They're fresh, tasty, and definitely worth the price. My sister and I stumbled upon this place by chance and the long line outside convinced us to give it a try. Despite the wait, the quality of the croissants blew us away. The only downside was the slow service - they really need more staff to help out with the orders. But trust me, it's totally worth the wait for these delicious croissants! Plus, they have other goodies like shirts, chocolates, and more. Check it out and satisfy your cravings! #BestCroissantsEver #WorthTheWait #DeliciousTreats 🛍️🍫

text.text:
I can see the popularity and hype of CAR.   There's usually a line on the weekends.  They are known for their croissants and specialty coffee drinks.   I got the madegascar vanilla latte and plain almond croissant.  Latte was delightful!  Lovely balance.
The croissant was fresh, flaky, and oh so good!!  Its up there with french pastries.  Highly recommend!  Worth waiting in line.
